fr.recommendations: Clearing and maintaining the existing dense vegetation around the property may enhance visibility and ease of movement for emergency responders, facilitating quicker access to critical areas. Clearly defined and well-maintained pedestrian walkways and multiple access points would support efficient movement and rapid deployment of personnel. Ensuring that the vehicle parking area and front driveway remain unobstructed could facilitate the movement of emergency vehicles and equipment, thus improving response times. Creating dedicated staging areas in the vicinity of the main building could further assist in organizing emergency operations. Prioritizing these enhancements would support effective navigation and operational coordination across the property.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing the separation between the tree cluster and the structure and managing vegetation directly adjacent to the building could improve defensible space and reduce potential structure exposure. Reducing vegetation continuity around scattered trees, particularly near the structure, would support separation efforts and improve wildfire resilience. Maintaining the vegetated perimeter with careful management to ensure it does not become overly dense may contribute to continued defensible space. Preserving and potentially extending the open fuel break could help limit fire spread across the property. While surrounding open space provides a buffer, improving side access by clearing any obstructive vegetation would support firefighter access and suppression activities. Continued assessment and strategic management of vegetation in these areas may promote long-term resilience and help safeguard the property from future wildfire exposure.
